explanation: The property states that if the system is being powered off (represented by the atomic proposition 'off'), then before this happens, both logging of key parameters and saving of those parameters ('logParams' and 'saveLog') must occur. This is captured by requiring that globally, whenever 'off' becomes true, it is implied that both 'logParams' and 'saveLog' were true at that moment or before. The chosen template expresses this causality which appropriately matches the requirement that logging and saving must be ensured before power-off.

The natural language requirement states that before the system is powered off, it should log key parameters and save them. By the atomic proposition descriptions, 'saveLog' directly expresses saving the log, and 'logParams' expresses logging parameters. To capture the requirement that both actions should happen before the system is powered off, the temporal property should require that it is always the case (globally, G) that if the system is about to be powered off, then 'logParams' and 'saveLog' have happened just before, or will be done as part of shutdown. Since there is no explicit proposition for 'off', but only 'powerButton' (pressed) or 'off' (system is de-energized) in APs, for safety, we encode: Whenever 'off' becomes true, it must be preceded by (or at the same time as) 'logParams' and 'saveLog'. This leads us to template 40, which ensures that whenever both 'logParams' and 'saveLog' are true (i.e., both logging and saving occurs), the system can be powered off. However, the original requirement is a bit more "A before B". To enforce that 'logParams' and 'saveLog' must both be true right before or with 'off', the best match is:
G((logParams & saveLog) -> off)
This means: it is always the case that if key parameters have been logged and saved, the system can be turned off. This enforces the logging/saving as a prerequisite to powering down. This corresponds to template 40.

The requirement states the system must log key parameters and save them before powering off. In the given atomic propositions, 'logParams' (log parameters) and 'saveLog' (save log of parameters) are the main actions. The sequence described is: logging (logParams & saveLog) must happen before the system is powered off ('off'). The appropriate LTL template is one that specifies that whenever the system is about to be powered off, it is required that logging and saving the log have occurred before or at that moment. This is best captured with the template '((! (bool_exp1) -> (((! ((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1))) U (((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1)) & (X bool_exp3)))) | (G (! ((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1)))))) & (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3)', which enforces that immediately prior to turning off, log and save must occur (with the minimal duration for the temporal window), and at all times when turning off, save and logging must have just occurred. Here, bool_exp1 = 'off', and bool_exp3 = 'logParams & saveLog'. N_DURATION is not needed for this template, so it is set as null.
chosen_LTL_template: (((! bool_exp1) -> (((! ((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1))) U (((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1)) & (X (! bool_exp3)))) | (G (! ((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1)))))) & (bool_exp1 -> (! bool_exp3)))

explanation: The requirement is that the system must log key parameters and save them before being powered off. In the provided atomic propositions, 'logParams' indicates logging parameters and 'saveLog' indicates saving the log of parameters. The natural language statement implies that before the system is powered off, both logging and saving should occur. Thus, whenever the system is turned off ('off' becomes true), it should be preceded (at least in the same step or before) by both logging and saving of parameters. The closest template to express this requirement is that it is always the case that if 'off' becomes true, then, at some point previously (or immediately), both 'logParams' and 'saveLog' were true. Given the templates, the most straightforward way is to say that, whenever 'off' occurs, it must be the case that both 'logParams' and 'saveLog' have occurred before or at the same time. Since past operators are not available, the best approximation with present/future LTL is to require that 'logParams' and 'saveLog' both happen before or at the same time as 'off' (i.e., 'off' implies both were true). This can be encoded as G (off -> (logParams & saveLog)). Using template 30, with bool_exp1=off, bool_exp3=(logParams & saveLog).

The requirement states that the system must perform two actions before being powered off: (1) log key parameters, and (2) save them. In the given atomic propositions, 'logParams' represents logging parameters and 'saveLog' corresponds to saving them. We want to ensure that before the system is powered off ('off' = true), both 'logParams' and 'saveLog' have become true (i.e., these actions have happened). The best fit among the templates is the one in which, globally, if the system turns off, it is required that both 'logParams' and 'saveLog' have previously occurred (in the same or earlier state). This is captured by: G (off -> (logParams & saveLog)), ensuring that the system never reaches the off state without having logged and saved the parameters first.

The requirement "The system shall log key parameters and save them before being powered off" implies that, before the system turns off, it should ensure that key parameters are both logged and saved. In the context of the available atomic propositions, 'logParams' (log parameters) and 'saveLog' (save log of parameters) are directly relevant, and 'off' (system is completely de-energized/off) indicates power-off. The correct order needs to guarantee that logging and saving happen before power-off.

The temporal pattern "Before event B, eventually property A must occur" corresponds to the LTL pattern: if not B, then (!B U (B & (X A))), or more simply, whenever not off, eventually on the point of turning off, both logParams and saveLog must have happened. The closest available template is 150, which enforces that on the transition from not-off to off, the logging and saving must take place right before that step.

Therefore, by setting bool_exp1 = off, and bool_exp3 = (logParams & saveLog), template 150 enforces that the system cannot go off unless logParams and saveLog have just happened; otherwise, the system can remain in not-off state.

The input requirement states that before the system powers off, it must log key parameters and save them. From the propositions, 'logParams' indicates logging parameters and 'saveLog' indicates saving them. The correct LTL property should ensure that whenever the system turns off (indicated by 'off'), it must be preceded (eventually before off) by both logging the parameters and saving the log. This captures the intent of the requirement that both logging and saving must occur before shutting down. The template '(G (bool_exp1 -> (F bool_exp3)))' matches the pattern 'whenever bool_exp1 (off) happens, bool_exp3 (logParams & saveLog) must eventually happen', but to ensure both actions happen before off, we use 'G (off -> (F (logParams & saveLog)))', but ideally, in more expressive logic, we should assert '(!off) U (logParams & saveLog)', followed by 'off'. This can be expressed here by insisting whenever off occurs, 'logParams & saveLog' must have already occurred, and the strongest invariant is to use 'G (off -> (logParams & saveLog))', but that enforces log and save at the same time as off. Given the templates, the closest is to use eventuality prior with template 19: 'G (bool_exp2 -> (F bool_exp3))', with bool_exp2 = 'off', and bool_exp3 = '(logParams & saveLog)'. N_DURATION is set to 5 as a reasonable small bound for liveness.

The natural language requirement is that the system must log key parameters and save them before being powered off. Here, 'logParams' represents logging key parameters and 'saveLog' indicates saving them. The action to take ('logParams & saveLog') should occur before the system is powered off ('off'). The most direct LTL encoding for this pattern is to require that it is always the case that if the system is about to turn off ('off' becomes true in the next step), then currently 'logParams & saveLog' must hold (i.e., it has just been performed before shutting down). This is best captured using the template: G(off -> (logParams & saveLog)).

The requirement specifies that before the system is powered off, it must both log key parameters and save them. From the available atomic propositions, 'logParams' indicates logging parameters, and 'saveLog' indicates saving the log of parameters. We want to specify that when the system is about to be powered off ('off' becomes true in the next state), these two actions must have just completed. The template that best captures the constraint 'before being powered off, log and save parameters' is one expressing that whenever the system is about to turn off in the next state, 'logParams' and 'saveLog' must be true in the current state. This is best captured by template 41: G ((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> (X bool_exp3)), where bool_exp1 is 'logParams', bool_exp2 is 'saveLog', and bool_exp3 is 'off'. This template ensures that every time both logging and saving occurs, off follows in the next state, enforcing the need to log and save before powering off.

The requirement states that key parameters should be logged and saved before the system is powered off. Here, 'logParams' (log parameters) and 'saveLog' (save log of parameters) capture logging and saving, while 'off' indicates system shutdown. The property is: whenever the system is about to be off, it must be the case that the parameters have been logged and the log has been saved beforehand. To guarantee logging and saving before power off, we can express that it is always the case that if off becomes true, then at some point previously logParams and saveLog were both true. The most fitting template is an 'always' implication: G (off -> (logParams & saveLog)), which captures that off is only allowed if both logging and saving have happened. This matches template 30 (G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3)), fitting the need for a single antecedent and a conjunction in the consequent. For timing, since the requirement says 'before', no specific window is required, but we assume the logging and saving happen prior to or concurrently with power off. N_DURATION is not needed for this template.
